<question>
1. a is lime.
2. a is west.
3. If a is east and a is lime, then b is cobalt.

9. If a is west and a is lime, then b is harbor.
10. If b is harbor, then b is north.
11. If b is north and b is slate, then c is laurel.
12. If c is laurel, then c is north.
13. If b is north and b is hazel, then c is slate.
14. If c is slate, then c is south.
15. If b is north and b is cobalt, then c is harbor.
16. If c is harbor, then c is south.
17. If b is north and b is harbor, then c is cobalt.
18. If c is cobalt, then c is south.
19. If c is north and c is laurel, then d is hazel.
20. If d is hazel, then d is south.
21. If c is south and c is cobalt, then d is harbor.
22. If d is harbor, then d is east.
23. If c is south and c is harbor, then d is laurel.
24. If d is laurel, then d is south.
25. If c is south and c is slate, then d is cobalt.
26. If d is cobalt, then d is east.
27. If d is south and d is hazel, then e is harbor.
28. If e is harbor, then e is west.
29. If d is south and d is laurel, then e is laurel.
30. If e is laurel, then e is east.
31. If d is east and d is harbor, then e is hazel.
32. If e is hazel, then e is east.
33. If d is east and d is cobalt, then e is slate.
34. If e is slate, then e is east.
35. If e is east and e is laurel, then f is cobalt.
36. If f is cobalt, then f is west.
37. If e is east and e is hazel, then f is laurel.
38. If f is laurel, then f is west.
39. If e is west and e is harbor, then f is hazel.
40. If f is hazel, then f is west.
41. If e is east and e is slate, then f is slate.
42. If f is slate, then f is west.
Which state applies to f?
</question>

<formal>
<constants>
a = a
b = b
c = c
d = d
e = e
f = f
</constants>
<predicates>
Ax: x is harbor
Bx: x is cobalt
Cx: x is laurel
Dx: x is slate
Ex: x is hazel
Fx: x is lime
Gx: x is north
Hx: x is south
Ix: x is east
Jx: x is west
</predicates>
<premises>
Fa
Ja
Ia & Fa -> Bb
Bb -> Gb
Ha & Fa -> Db
Db -> Gb
Ga & Fa -> Eb
Eb -> Gb
Ja & Fa -> Ab
Ab -> Gb
Gb & Db -> Cc
Cc -> Gc
Gb & Eb -> Dc
Dc -> Hc
Gb & Bb -> Ac
Ac -> Hc
Gb & Ab -> Bc
Bc -> Hc
Hb & Ab -> Bc
Bc -> Hc
Hc & Ac -> Ad
Ad -> Id
Hc & Ab -> Cd
Cd -> Id
Hc & Dc -> Bd
Bd -> Id
Id & Hd -> Ed
Ed -> Id
Id & Bd -> De
De -> Id
</premises>
<proof>
Fa ; R
Ja ; R
Ab ; ->E
Gb ; ->E
Bc ; ->E
Hc ; ->E
Ad ; ->E
Id ; ->E
Ed ; ->E
Id ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Id
</conclusion>
</formal>
<answer>
hazel
</answer>

<question>
1. a is granite.
2. a is north.
3. If a is north and a is granite, then b is olive.
4. If b is olive, then b is north.
5. If a is west and a is granite, then b is harbor.
6. If b is harbor, then b is north.
7. If a is south and a is granite, then b is cedar.
8. If b is cedar, then b is north.
9. If a is east and a is granite, then b is birch.
10. If b is birch, then b is north.
11. If b is north and b is harbor, then c is olive.
12. If c is olive, then c is south.
13. If b is north and b is birch, then c is cedar.
14. If c is cedar, then c is south.
15. If b is north and b is cedar, then c is harbor.
16. If c is harbor, then c is south.
17. If b is north and b is olive, then c is birch.
18. If c is birch, then c is south.
19. If c is south and c is olive, then d is cedar.
20. If d is cedar, then d is east.
21. If c is south and c is harbor, then d is olive.
22. If d is olive, then d is east.
23. If c is south and c is birch, then d is harbor.
24. If d is harbor, then d is east.
25. If c is south and c is cedar, then d is birch.
26. If d is birch, then d is east.
Which state applies to d?
</question>

<formal>
<constants>
a = a
b = b
c = c
d = d
</constants>
<predicates>
Ax: x is cedar
Bx: x is harbor
Cx: x is birch
Dx: x is granite
Ex: x is olive
Fx: x is north
Gx: x is south
Hx: x is east
Ix: x is west
</predicates>
<premises>
Da
Fa
Fa & Da -> Eb
Eb -> Fb
Ia & Da -> Bb
Bb -> Fb
Ga & Da -> Ab
Ab -> Fb
Ha & Da -> Cb
Cb -> Fb
Fb & Bb -> Ec
Ec -> Gc
Fb & Cb -> Ac
Ac -> Gc
Fb & Ab -> Bc
Bc -> Gc
Fb & Eb -> Cc
Cc -> Gc
Gc & Ec -> Ad
Ad -> Hd
Gc & Bc -> Ed
Ed -> Hd
Gc & Cc -> Bd
Bd -> Hd
Gc & Ac -> Cd
Cd -> Hd
</premises>
<proof>
Da ; R
Fa ; R
Eb ; ->E
Fb ; ->E
Ec ; ->E
Gc ; ->E
Ad ; ->E
Hd ; ->E
</proof>
<conclusion>
Hd
</conclusion>
</formal>
<answer>
cedar
</answer>
